title: Humiriastrum diguense
taxonomy: Humiriastrum diguense is classified within the family Humiriaceae. This family is a part of the order Malpighiales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Malpighiales is a subset of the clade Rosidae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The clade Rosidae is a part of the superclade Rosid II, which is also known as the 'rosid clade'. The rosid clade is a major clade of flowering plants.

intro: Humiriastrum diguense is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Humiriaceae and commonly known as 'Diguena tree'. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which can help to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Humiriastrum diguense include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Humiriastrum diguense is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, as well as being used in the form of a poultice or cream to treat various health conditions.
